response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior engineer with nearly two decades of hands-on experience, deep open-source involvement, and impressive technical breadth spanning web, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems on Linux. However, the role explicitly requires German at minimum C1, and the resume lists German as 'Basic' — a hard disqualifier for a client-facing leadership position at a German consultancy. Additionally, while he founded an open-source project and has mentored through workshops, there is no formal track record of leading teams or holding executive/head-of-development responsibility, and his São Paulo location complicates the domestic-travel expectation.
strengths: Extensive, diverse software engineering experience (web, embedded, high-scale backends) with strong Linux and open-source depth
Founder of an open-source geo-data platform plus active community teaching, workshops, and conference talks
Demonstrated software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) aligned with consulting quality standards
concerns: German listed as 'Basic' — role requires minimum C1 for client advisory work
No formal team-leadership or executive management experience; career is IC-track senior/staff roles
Based in São Paulo, which conflicts with the remote-first-plus-domestic-travel model of a German consultancy

key_factors.factor: Broad senior engineering and open-source/Linux expertise
Lack of formal leadership/executive track record
